title: BC FLA Separation Agreement — Child Support and Spousal Support Sub-Schema

source_name.description: Name or category of income source (e.g., employment, self-employment, investment, pension, disability benefits).

annual_amount.description: Gross annual amount from this source in CAD.

taxable.description: Whether this income source is taxable under the Income Tax Act, R.S.C. 1985, c. 1 (5th Supp.).

grossed_up_amount.description: If non-taxable, the grossed-up equivalent for guideline income purposes per Federal Child Support Guidelines s. 19(1)(h).

documentation.description: Document substantiating this source (e.g., T4, T4A, T5, financial statement).

amount.description: Payment amount per period in CAD.

frequency.description: How often the payment is made.

payment_due_date.description: Day or date within each period payment is due (e.g., '1st of each month').

payment_method.description: Method of payment. FMEP refers to BC Family Maintenance Enforcement Program enrollment.

trigger_event.description: Event that triggers a support review (e.g., child completes secondary school, payor reaches age 65, recipient achieves self-sufficiency).

anticipated_date.description: Estimated date of trigger if ascertainable.

review_process.description: Dispute resolution process to be followed upon trigger.

category.description: Category of special or extraordinary expense under Federal Child Support Guidelines s. 7. [VERIFY: s. 7 categories]

annual_amount_estimated.description: Estimated gross annual cost in CAD.

tax_deduction_credit.description: Value of any tax deduction or credit attributable to this expense in CAD.

net_after_tax.description: Net annual cost after applying tax deductions or credits.

proportional_share_payor.description: Payor's proportional share of net expense, based on ratio of respective guideline incomes.

proportional_share_recipient.description: Recipient's proportional share of net expense.

documentation_required.description: Documents required to substantiate expense (e.g., receipts, invoices, enrollment confirmation).

income_determination.description: Income determination for each spouse for guideline support purposes.

line_15000_income_prior_year.description: Total income per CRA T1 General, line 15000, for the most recent completed tax year.

line_15000_income_current_estimate.description: Estimated current-year total income per line 15000 if materially different from prior year.

imputed_income.description: Income imputed under Federal Child Support Guidelines s. 19, if applicable.

imputed_amount.description: Amount of income imputed in CAD.

basis.description: Statutory basis for imputation (e.g., s. 19(1)(a) intentionally under-employed, s. 19(1)(f) exempt from tax). [VERIFY: s. 19 subsections]

self_employment_adjustments.description: Adjustments to self-employment income per Federal Child Support Guidelines ss. 17-18 (add back deductions not reflecting actual cash available). [VERIFY: ss. 17-18 scope]

disallowed_deductions.description: Deductions added back to income (e.g., CCA, personal vehicle, home office).

non_taxable_income.description: Non-taxable income (e.g., disability benefits, workers' compensation) and grossed-up equivalent.

total_guideline_income.description: Final guideline income for support calculation purposes.

prescribed_income_documentation.description: Documents exchanged or to be exchanged per Federal Child Support Guidelines s. 21 (e.g., T1 General, T4, Notice of Assessment, corporate financial statements). [VERIFY: s. 21 prescribed documents]

required.description: Whether ongoing annual disclosure is required.

form_of_disclosure.description: What must be provided (e.g., 'complete copy of T1 General, all T4/T4A/T5 slips, Notice of Assessment, and current financial statement if self-employed').

child_support.description: Child support terms under the Federal Child Support Guidelines, SOR/97-175, and BC Family Law Act, S.B.C. 2011, c. 25, Part 7.

payor.description: The spouse obligated to pay child support.

payor_guideline_income.description: Payor's guideline income for table amount lookup.

recipient_guideline_income.description: Recipient's guideline income, required for shared or split parenting arrangements under s. 8 or s. 9.

child_of_marriage.description: Whether child qualifies as child of the marriage per Divorce Act, R.S.C. 1985, c. 3 (2nd Supp.), s. 2(1), or child under FLA s. 146. [VERIFY: FLA s. 146 definition of child for support]

children.description: Children for whom support is payable.

parenting_arrangement_for_cs.description: Parenting arrangement classification affecting calculation: primary (s. 3), shared (s. 9, each parent 40%+ time), split (s. 8).

table_amount.description: Monthly table amount per applicable Federal Tables, BC schedule.

set_off_calculation.description: Set-off calculation for shared or split parenting under s. 8 or s. 9.

set_off_amount.description: Difference between the two table amounts.

additional_factors_considered.description: Any s. 9(b)-(c) factors (increased costs of shared parenting, conditions/means/needs).

fmep_enrollment.description: Whether the order/agreement is enrolled with the BC Family Maintenance Enforcement Program.

end_event.description: Event upon which child support terminates (e.g., 'child ceases to be a child of the marriage per Divorce Act s. 2(1) or is no longer a child under FLA s. 146'). [VERIFY: FLA s. 146 and s. 147 re child support obligation end]

section_7_expenses.description: Special or extraordinary expenses under Federal Child Support Guidelines s. 7.

reconciliation_cadence.description: How often s. 7 expenses are reconciled (e.g., 'quarterly with receipts within 30 days of quarter end').

pre_approval_required.description: Whether both spouses must consent before incurring a new s. 7 expense.

retroactive_child_support.description: Retroactive child support claim, resolved per DBS v. SRG, 2006 SCC 37, and Michel v. Graydon, 2020 SCC 24. [VERIFY: Michel v. Graydon citation 2020 SCC 24]

claimed.description: Whether a retroactive claim was made.

resolved.description: Whether the retroactive claim is fully resolved in this agreement.

period_covered.description: Period for which retroactive support is owed.

amount_agreed.description: Total retroactive amount agreed in CAD.

payment_plan.description: Terms for paying the retroactive amount (e.g., lump sum by date, instalments).

spousal_support.description: Spousal support terms under FLA Part 7, Division 8, and the Spousal Support Advisory Guidelines (SSAG).

entitlement_basis.description: Basis of entitlement per Moge v. Moge, [1992] 3 SCR 813, and Bracklow v. Bracklow, [1999] 1 SCR 420.

applicable_guideline.description: Advisory framework used for quantum and duration.

range_low_duration_months.description: Low-end duration in months (null if indefinite).

agreed_position.description: Where the agreed amount and duration fall relative to the SSAG range.

reasons_for_position.description: Explanation if amount/duration is above or below the SSAG range.

review_event.description: Event triggering end or review if type is to_review_event.

index_reference.description: Index used (e.g., 'Statistics Canada CPI All-Items, Vancouver, B.C.').

adjustment_cadence.description: When adjustment takes effect (e.g., 'January 1 of each year').

lump_sum_option.description: Lump sum spousal support in lieu of or in addition to periodic payments.

present_value_discount_rate.description: Discount rate used for present value calculation.

tax_characterization.description: Tax treatment: generally not deductible to payor nor includable to recipient for lump sums per ITA.

waiver.description: Waiver of spousal support, structured to withstand Miglin v. Miglin, 2003 SCC 24, Stage 1 and Stage 2 analysis.

acknowledgment_compensatory_entitlement_.description: Acknowledgment that the waiving spouse had or may have had a compensatory entitlement and the reasons for waiving it.

acknowledgment_change_of_conditions.description: Acknowledgment that conditions may change but the waiver is intended to be final and binding.

acknowledgment_ila_on_waiver.description: Confirmation that the waiving spouse received independent legal advice specifically on the spousal support waiver.

reasons_for_waiver.description: Reasons supporting the waiver (e.g., both spouses self-sufficient, short marriage, equalized by property division).

life_insurance.description: Life insurance policy maintained to secure support obligations.

beneficiary_designation.description: Irrevocable beneficiary designation (e.g., recipient spouse in trust for children).

term.description: Policy term (e.g., 'until youngest child ceases to be child of marriage and spousal support terminates').

proof_of_coverage_cadence.description: How often proof of coverage must be provided (e.g., 'annually by January 31').

charge_on_property.description: Charge registered against real property as security for support arrears or lump sum.

fmep_assignment.description: Whether the agreement is assigned to the BC Family Maintenance Enforcement Program for enforcement.

support_review_and_variation.description: Review and variation provisions for child and spousal support.

scheduled_review_dates.description: Specific future dates on which support will be reviewed.

automatic_review_triggers.description: Events that automatically trigger a support review.

material_change_threshold.description: Definition of material change in circumstances sufficient to warrant variation (e.g., 'a change in income of 10% or more, or any change that, had it been known, would likely have resulted in a different order per Willick v. Willick, [1994] 3 SCR 670').

dispute_resolution_process.description: Ordered sequence of dispute resolution steps before court application. FLA s. 4 encourages out-of-court resolution.

tax_treatment.description: Income tax treatment of support payments under the Income Tax Act, R.S.C. 1985, c. 1 (5th Supp.).

child_support_tax_treatment.description: Post-May 1997 child support: not deductible by payor, not includable by recipient, per ITA s. 56.1(4) and s. 60.1(4).

spousal_support_periodic_tax_treatment.description: Periodic spousal support: deductible by payor under ITA s. 60(b), includable by recipient under ITA s. 56(1)(b). [VERIFY: s. 60(b) and s. 56(1)(b) current references]

lump_sum_spousal_support_tax_treatment.description: Lump sum spousal support: generally not deductible to payor and not includable to recipient.

retroactive_support_tax_treatment.description: Retroactive periodic spousal support may be deductible/includable in the years to which it relates; recipient may request CRA reassessment for prior years per ITA s. 60.1(3). Retroactive child support is not deductible/includable. [VERIFY: s. 60.1(3) applicability]
